symbols.description: Two circular sigil drawings side by side at the bottom of the page. Left sigil: circle divided by a cross/plus into four quadrants, each containing small circles (3-4 per quadrant), with a small mark or numeral at the top center inside the circle. Right sigil: more complex circular composition with a central column/tree motif, what appears to be small human figures (a larger standing figure and smaller figures), an architectural element resembling a gate or temple with pillars/columns, small circles in the lower corners (possibly wheels or rosettes), and dots/sand-like texture at the bottom.
